workshop_pitch: You selected Monstrous Other plus Grieving Zealot and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is reproduction. Their primary moral texture is burden shifting. Also, on a completely ordinary Tuesday, they would absolutely be caught never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
opening_image: The real threat is not violence but the institutional permission structure surrounding it.
dossier_summary: This build runs on contagion inside a nest. At the story level, it violates the body or category boundary itself. The primary moral texture is burden shifting. The internal lie is simple: the species was always temporary. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of burden shifting who is still the kind of person who is never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional and expecting gratitude for it.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional. This behavior is now being interpreted internally as a visible instance of burden shifting. Staff morale is not to be interpreted as material risk at this time.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the nest where reproduction briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: turns private loss into public doctrine. Somewhere in the background, they are also the sort of person who would be never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by generic, one monstrous other build will learn that harm can be limited but not fully undone. Also, somebody is definitely never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional in act one and that should have been the warning.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
sample_lines: If never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional bothers you, wait until you see what I do with actual power.
You are not dealing with a person anymore. You are dealing with a permission structure.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: The villain is less interested in victory than in teaching the room a worse definition of normal.
Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn it keeps getting away with never volunteering for the cleanup work that keeps the place functional.
